_comment: Known security advisories for the agents agentscan scans. Used by the version/CVE check. Drawn from PUBLIC 2026 reporting (SonicWall, SoCRadar, ProArch, jgamblin/OpenClawCVEs, national CERTs). Feed-updatable via `agentscan --update-feed`. OpenClaw uses a DATE-BASED version scheme (YYYY.M.P, e.g. 2026.6.6) — fixedIn ranges below use that scheme so comparisons are meaningful. The check reports UNCERTAIN rather than false-alarming when it can't parse a version.

advisories.type: Cross-site WebSocket hijacking (CSWSH) → auth-token theft → RCE
advisories.summary: Local WebSocket server did not validate the Origin header; a malicious webpage could connect to localhost, steal the gateway auth token (no rate limiting on local connections), register as a trusted device, and achieve remote code execution.
A token rotation race condition let any legitimately paired device escalate to full admin access and achieve remote code execution.
Cluster of three RCE issues in the 7.1–7.3 CVSS band reported across 2026; upgrade to a patched release.
